label: Nyhetsbrev — Mailchimp
summary: Newsletter subscription form that POSTs to a Next.js Route Handler and adds the email to a Mailchimp audience. Client owns form state + UX (loading/success/already-subscribed/error); server route owns the Mailchimp REST call + validation. Degrades gracefully (mock: success): with no real MAILCHIMP_API_KEY (missing or an F2 preview stub) the route returns 200 { ok:true, demo:true } and the form shows a 'demo — not really subscribed' notice; a real key but missing MAILCHIMP_AUDIENCE_ID returns 503 with a 'configure newsletter' banner so F3 build does not crash.
summarySv: Anmälningsformulär för nyhetsbrev som lägger till besökarens e-postadress i din lista hos Mailchimp. Utan riktiga nycklar körs demoläge: anmälan går igenom med tydlig demomarkering, men ingen läggs till på riktigt förrän nycklarna är på plats.

envVars.purpose: Mailchimp REST API key. Mailchimp → Account & Billing → Extras → API keys. Format '<hex>-us21' where suffix = data-center id. Tagged 'feature-runtime': when missing or a preview stub the route returns a demo success (mock: success) instead of subscribing, so F3 build does not crash.
ID of the audience (formerly 'list') new subscribers are added to. Find it in Mailchimp under Audience → Settings → Audience name and defaults. Same 503-fallback pattern as MAILCHIMP_API_KEY.

Optional override for the Mailchimp data-center subdomain (e.g. 'us21'). When unset, the route derives it from the API key suffix (the '-us21' part). Only set this if your key format is unusual or you proxy Mailchimp through a custom host.
